title: Shops on UK High Streets Selling Illegal Drugs
summary: BBC secret filming found that some shops on UK High Streets are selling illegal drugs like cocaine and cannabis. Researchers went into shops in four towns in the West Midlands and were able to buy drugs easily. One street was called 'lawless' by a police source. The National Crime Agency says organized criminal gangs are using these shops to sell drugs. A senior MP wants the government to act quickly to stop this. The government says it is working with police to take action against these criminal businesses. Legitimate businesses nearby said they have been scared by gangs and have seen violence with knives and guns.

keywords.explanation: drugs that are not allowed by law, like cocaine and cannabis
small shops that sell everyday items like food and drinks
groups of people who work together to commit crimes
the main street in a town where many shops are located
people who pretend to be normal customers to find out what is really happening

background_read: Shops on High Streets usually sell food, drinks, and other everyday items. But this investigation found that some shops are also selling illegal drugs. The police and government are working to stop this. It is important for communities to be safe.
Article_Structure: WHO: BBC researchers and organized criminal gangs
WHAT: Mini-marts selling illegal drugs like cocaine and cannabis
WHERE: High Streets in four towns in the West Midlands, UK
WHY: Criminal gangs are using shops to sell drugs, and the government needs to act

perspectives.description: Police are working with partners to act on complaints about illegal drug sales.
A senior MP wants the government to act urgently to stop organized crime on High Streets.
